label: 948b Military commissions generally

Ex. Ord. No. 13425. Trial of Alien Unlawful Enemy Combatants by Military CommissionEx. Ord. No. 13425, , 72 F.R. 7737, provided:Feb. 14, 2007By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including the Military Commissions Act of 2006 (), the Authorization for Use of Military Force (), and , United States Code, it is hereby ordered as follows:Public Law 109–366Public Law 107–40section 948b(b) of title 101. . There are hereby established military commissions to try alien unlawful enemy combatants for offenses triable by military commission as provided in chapter 47A of title 10.SectionEstablishment of Military Commissions. 2. . As used in this order:SecDefinitions(a) “unlawful enemy combatant” has the meaning provided for that term in ; andsection 948a(1) of title 10(b) “alien” means a person who is not a citizen of the United States.. 3. . This order supersedes any provision of the President’s Military Order of (66 . 57,833), that relates to trial by military commission, specifically including:SecSupersedureFed. RegNovember 13, 2001(a) section 4 of the Military Order; and(b) any requirement in section 2 of the Military Order, as it relates to trial by military commission, for a determination of:(i) reason to believe specified matters; or(ii) the interest of the United States.. 4. . (a) This order shall be implemented in accordance with applicable law and subject to the availability of appropriations.SecGeneral Provisions(b) The heads of executive departments and agencies shall provide such information and assistance to the Secretary of Defense as may be necessary to implement this order and chapter 47A of title 10.(c) This order is not intended to, and does not, create any right or benefit, substantive or procedural, enforceable at law or in equity by any party against the United States, its departments, agencies, entities, officers, employees, or agents, or any other person.George W. Bush.

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: a .—Purpose
children.text: This chapter establishes procedures governing the use of military commissions to try alien unprivileged enemy belligerents for violations of the law of war and other offenses triable by military commission.

children.heading: .—Authority for Military Commissions Under This Chapter
children.label: b .—Authority for Military Commissions Under This Chapter
children.text: The President is authorized to establish military commissions under this chapter for offenses triable by military commission as provided in this chapter.

children.heading: .—Construction of Provisions
children.label: c .—Construction of Provisions
children.text: The procedures for military commissions set forth in this chapter are based upon the procedures for trial by general courts-martial under chapter 47 of this title (the Uniform Code of Military Justice). Chapter 47 of this title does not, by its terms, apply to trial by military commission except as specifically provided therein or in this chapter, and many of the provisions of chapter 47 of this title are by their terms inapplicable to military commissions. The judicial construction and application of chapter 47 of this title, while instructive, is therefore not of its own force binding on military commissions established under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: Section 810 (article 10 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice), relating to speedy trial, including any rule of courts-martial relating to speedy trial.

children.label: B
children.text: Sections 831(a), (b), and (d) (articles 31(a), (b), and (d) of the Uniform Code of Military Justice), relating to compulsory self-incrimination.

children.label: C
children.text: Section 832 (article 32 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice), relating to preliminary hearing.
Other provisions of chapter 47 of this title shall apply to trial by military commission under this chapter only to the extent provided by the terms of such provisions or by this chapter.

children.heading: .—Geneva Conventions Not Establishing Private Right of Action
children.label: e .—Geneva Conventions Not Establishing Private Right of Action
children.text: No alien unprivileged enemy belligerent subject to trial by military commission under this chapter may invoke the Geneva Conventions as a basis for a private right of action.
